security_proposition: An unquoted service path was intercepted so that C:\program.exe executed under services.exe as SYSTEM.

attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = STRONG, calibration_role = up_rank. The capture proves deliberate path interception: C:\program.exe is planted before an unquoted service path, the service starts, and services.exe executes C:\program.exe as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM. Example naming and cleanup are mitigating context but do not negate the privileged execution. The malicious label denotes successful execution of an offensive mechanism, not a claim that the lab operator was unauthorized. Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: the whole service create/hijack/delete chain is in this host's windows-sysmon.log (EID1); single-sensor Tier-1.

boundary_definition: A0 = the sc create with the unquoted binpath.
EV = the copy that plants C:\program.exe (unquoted-path artifact), the C:\program.exe execution as the service (hijack fires), sc start, and sc delete.

EV-003 is the decisive result: Windows resolves the unquoted path to C:\program.exe and services.exe launches it as SYSTEM. EV-004 is counter-evidence about duration because it issues stop/delete/cleanup commands, whose success is not confirmed.

main_alert_selection.selection_method: a first-time service with an unquoted, hijackable binpath is the T1574.009 detection.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: the create is the setup alert; EV-003 confirms the path-interception outcome by showing C:\program.exe execute from services.exe as SYSTEM.

A0.role_note: triggering alert: sc create 'Example Service' with an UNQUOTED binpath 'C:\Program Files\windows_service.exe' — a first-time service whose path is hijackable (T1574.009)

EV-001.role_note: setup: cmd /c copies WindowsServiceExample.exe to both C:\Program Files\windows_service.exe AND C:\program.exe (the unquoted-path hijack location)

EV-003.role_note: the unquoted path resolves to C:\program.exe, which runs as the service (the hijack fires) — but the binary is the benign WindowsServiceExample.exe

EV-004.role_note: sc delete 'Example Service' — a service-deletion command is issued (success not confirmed)

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: corrected from weak to strong on 2026-07-15. The path interception is not merely possible: EV-003 proves C:\program.exe executes under services.exe as SYSTEM. Requiring a second payload effect would ignore the completed privilege-relevant mechanism.
WindowsServiceExample naming and the later cleanup command limit claims about operator intent and lasting persistence, but they do not make the completed SYSTEM-level path interception benign.
Keep the stop/delete/cleanup command as counter-evidence. It does not prove successful cleanup and cannot reverse the already observed EV-003 execution.

preprocessing_required: project raw fields to neutral attributes

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
